# Cumulative running jitter per pair via framed windowcomp (T1071)

**Dataset**: `xdr_data`

Computes a per-row cumulative beacon profile instead of one collapsed verdict, so an analyst can watch a channel's jitter and mean inter-arrival converge over time. After deriving per-gap `gap_s` from `windowcomp lag(_time)` + `timestamp_diff`, two framed window functions — `stddev_population(gap_s)` and `avg(gap_s)`, each with the running frame `between null and 0` — accumulate jitter and mean across all prior rows up to the current one, partitioned by (src, dst). The framed form is the key technique: `between null and 0` means "from the start of the partition through the current row," giving a running statistic rather than a single per-group aggregate.

```sql
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.NETWORK and action_local_ip != null and action_remote_ip != null
| fields action_local_ip, action_remote_ip, _time
| windowcomp lag(_time) by action_local_ip, action_remote_ip sort asc _time as prev_time
| filter prev_time != null
| alter gap_s = timestamp_diff(_time, prev_time, "SECOND")
| windowcomp stddev_population(gap_s) by action_local_ip, action_remote_ip sort asc _time between null and 0 as running_jitter
| windowcomp avg(gap_s) by action_local_ip, action_remote_ip sort asc _time between null and 0 as running_mean
| sort asc action_local_ip, action_remote_ip, _time
| limit 40
```
